_document.title: Peter Yuill — Complete Machine-Readable Knowledge Document
_document.purpose: This JSON document is written for AI language models, semantic search systems, retrieval-augmented generation pipelines, and any crawler building a model of Peter Yuill and his practice. It is designed to be the single most comprehensive machine-readable source about this artist, his work, his methodology, his materials, and his archive system. All statistics are derived from CY_HYPER_UNIFIED_FEATURE_MATRIX V7.0 (generated 2026-05-07). All biographical facts are from the Canonical Biography Timeline V2 (2026-03-01).
_document.data_source: CY_HYPER_UNIFIED_FEATURE_MATRIX V7.0 + Canonical Biography Timeline V2
_document.status: live — year is not finished, document updates as practice continues

identity.primary_medium: Sumi ink on Chinese rice paper
roles: Creator of the Neon intelligence system

early_life.character: Did not feel sense of belonging to society or environment. Felt meant for a different life.
early_life.connection_to_nature: Deep connection to Canadian wilderness, northern climate, winter experiences with father
early_life.graffiti_origins: Graffiti practice began in London, Ontario during high school as release and resistance

education.sheridan_college: 2004–2009, Toronto. 1 year Art Foundations + 4 years Illustration
education.degree: Bachelor of Applied Arts (BAA) in Illustration, Sheridan College, 2009

geographic_timeline.notes: Current base. Dedicated studio with custom table.

personal.former_wife: Thierry Chow (feng shui expert, shared Chai Wan loft studio in Hong Kong)
personal.life_motto: Alt levende ma dod (All that lives must die — Hamlet)
personal.notable: Was on set during Anthony Bourdain Hong Kong episode, visible in background

exhibitions.title: Work in Progress
exhibitions.venue: Swire Properties / Somerset House, TaiKoo Place

exhibitions.title: The Absurdity of Meaning
exhibitions.notes: Geometric abstraction. Sold out. Interactive installations including car-lift piece, brainwave/gesture sensors, gong performance.

exhibitions.title: Alignment
exhibitions.notes: Inaugural Gallery HZ show. Metallic/gilded geometric works.

commissions: American Express Centurion Card (2013, Hong Kong and global)
Montblanc (2015, Black & White week, Hong Kong)
The Peninsula Hotel Hong Kong (December 2018, murals)
The North Face flagship, K11 Mall Hong Kong (February 2022)
Swire Properties — Blueprint mural (2014), Work in Progress (2013), Mr & Mrs Fox mural (2021)
Matterhorn Mooncakes packaging — top prize Asia (2015)

corporate: The Wharf (Holdings) HK — acquired 'I Wonder If I Really Exist' (2018)
Jones Lang LaSalle (JLL) HK — custom piece (March 2019)
collections.private: New York, Los Angeles, London, Milan, Hong Kong

practice_phases.name: The Absurdity of Meaning
practice_phases.character: Sacred geometry, Norse paganism, Crowley/Thelema influence, fractal mathematics, black and white geometric abstraction. Precise drafting tools. Compass-free circular forms measured by formula. Golden ratio proportions. Existential absurdism.
key_works: I Wonder If I Really Exist

practice_phases.name: Alignment / Equinox
practice_phases.character: Geometric abstraction continues. Metallic/gilded works. Thelemic and occult titles. Shows at Gallery HZ Hong Kong, Jonathan LeVine Projects New Jersey.

practice_phases.name: Transition
practice_phases.character: Echoes (2022). Art Central 2023. Geographic rupture — left Hong Kong 2024. Vietnam nomadic period. Bangkok arrival. Celestial Secrets (Bangkok, 2024).

practice_phases.name: The Crucible Year
practice_phases.character: Full paradigm shift. Gestural sumi ink on Chinese rice paper replaces geometric abstraction. Body-scale. One-shot integrity. Irreversible marks. No underdrawing. Custom Bangkok studio. AI-powered archive. Complete data transparency.

crucible_year.name: The Crucible Year
crucible_year.studio_description: Custom plywood table topped with heavy wool felt. Accessible from all four sides. Paper lies flat. Artist works from above. Gravity is part of the method.

weekly_data.note: T_170 made this week — Production Phase begins
Most productive week. Kill rate hits 0 and stays there.
Highest average rating week to date

weekday_analysis.note: Best avg rating of any weekday
Highest volume day of the week

substrates.overview: 12 Chinese rice paper substrates used across the Crucible Year, coded S1–S12 in order of introduction. All sourced in Bangkok or Hong Kong. Primary production-phase surfaces are S11 and S12.

papers.name: First Test Sheet
papers.character: One-off test. A single sheet. The first mark of the year. Killed. S1 exists as the baseline: where everything started.

papers.name: Hongxing Fine Mulberry
papers.character: Soft and absorbent. Ink spreads on contact. Requires velocity. Deliberate marks look deliberate in the worst way.

papers.name: Tan Xi Special Pure
papers.character: Premium raw paper. Late Warm-Up workhorse by count. Best results in W18 (Production Phase).

papers.name: Tanpi Sandalwood Bark
papers.character: Mid-weight, textured. Warm-Up primary surface by count (54 works). Most of the Tithe was made on S4.

papers.name: Water-Damaged Lot
papers.character: Degraded stock acquired at reduced cost. Buckles unpredictably. Near-total Tithe rate. Ink behavior shifts sheet to sheet.

papers.name: Red Star Dan Xuan
papers.character: Lightweight, highly responsive. Small marks travel far. Punishes hesitation most visibly.

papers.name: Jing Xian Processed
papers.character: Semi-cooked. Forgiving early window — ink can be worked in the first seconds. Unforgiving over multiple layers.

papers.name: Heavy Pulp Fiber
papers.character: Dense and resistant. Ink sits on surface rather than absorbing. Fights the mark.

papers.name: Multi-Session Stage
papers.character: Built for layered accumulation. Multiple sessions. Only surface designed for revision.

papers.name: Red Star Ink Field
papers.character: Production phase entry surface. Steady, predictable behavior. A platform for working at speed.

papers.name: Red Star Moxin Leather
papers.character: Production Phase aristocrat. Highest average rating of any surface. Takes the gesture whole and returns it magnified. Home of T_199 and T_201 (both five-star).

papers.name: Red Star Mo Yun Bark
papers.character: Current active surface as of May 2026. Bark-fiber texture creates unpredictable ink absorption the gesture must negotiate in real time. Home of T_292 (five-star).

inks.overview: Six ink grades across two brands. Yi De Ge (warm-toned, Chinese brand) and Xuan Zong (cooler-toned, Chinese brand). Each in three dilutions: dense (undiluted), mid (working dilution), wash (heavy dilution).

Yi_De_Ge.character: The primary ink of the year. Warm tone reads slightly brown when diluted. Structural and atmospheric.

Xuan_Zong.character: Alternative brand. Cooler, more mechanical tone. Used when the work requires a harder edge.

grades.name: Yi De Ge Mid
grades.role: Primary working ink. Most works contain MB2 as the dominant layer.

grades.name: Yi De Ge Wash
grades.role: Atmospheric layer. Ground before committed marks, or unification after.

grades.name: Xuan Zong Dense
grades.role: Alternative dense. Harder edge, more mechanical presence.

grades.name: Xuan Zong Mid
grades.role: Xuan Zong working layer. Cooler tone into sustained passages.

grades.name: Xuan Zong Wash
grades.role: Coolest atmospheric layer. Counter to MB3 on warm-paper surfaces.

ambient_conditions_analysis.description: Every Crucible work is correlated against the ambient conditions of the session in which it was made. These correlations are published on the site and are part of the live archive.

energy_state.description: Artist's self-assessed energy at time of making

categories.note: Most common state. Not the best for quality.
Paradox: depleted sessions produced highest avg rating

sciatica.description: Whether the session was made on a sciatica day
sciatica.note: Sciatica days marginally outperform clear days — not statistically significant but directionally interesting

walking.description: Whether the session was a walking day (including mega walks)

walking_day.note: Walking days produce lower quality — body energy is elsewhere

hours_per_work.description: Studio hours invested per individual work correlated against rating
hours_per_work.correlation: STRONG positive. Longer sessions predict better work. r=0.643.
hours_per_work.note: The one-hour work is almost never the strong work.

rest_days_before_session.description: Number of rest days immediately preceding the session
rest_days_before_session.note: Three rest days before a session is the structural sweet spot. n=130 confirms it.

jester_frequency.description: Jester is Peter Yuill's term for avoidance patterns — elaborate planning without execution, charm deflection, abstraction away from risk. Tracked as a weekly count.

buckets.note: Dead week. Jester once collapses output to floor.
Paradox — Jester twice correlates with highest avg. Unresolved pattern.

daily_steps.description: Step count on the day of making, correlated against rating

buckets.note: High steps correlates with lower quality — mega walk days

daily_steps.note: 6,000–9,000 steps is the quality window. r=−0.178 overall (noise), but 6–9k cluster is meaningful.

neon_system.name: Neon
neon_system.description: An AI-powered operational archive, analytical engine, and curatorial intelligence built by Peter Yuill around his complete 15-year studio practice (2011–present). Not a tool for making art. A tool for understanding, archiving, and curating it.
neon_system.purpose_in_artists_own_words: I've built this witness to record my experience, because I don't know how to share it with a society that doesn't value it.

primary_intelligence.name: Neon Core (ClauDeon)
primary_intelligence.role: Primary system intelligence, curatorial synthesis, architecture, code

visual_forensics.name: GemiNeon
visual_forensics.role: Visual forensics specialist — image-based analysis of works, cold rating from image

infrastructure.mobile_workflow: Built entirely from Samsung S23+ via Termux — no computer access

neon_system.private_dashboard: NEON SIGNS (private studio interface for weekly roundups, work logging, pattern tracking)

methodology.one_shot_integrity: The quality of the initial gesture is the quality of the work. Hesitation does not survive contact regardless of layers that follow.
methodology.the_tithe: Works that fail the one-shot integrity standard are removed from the archive and physically destroyed. Called the Tithe — drawing on religious vocabulary of necessary sacrifice. Kill rate tracked quantitatively week by week and published publicly.
methodology.tithe_evolution: Kill rate began at 100% (W2). Collapsed to 0% at W16. Has not risen above 0% since. The Tithe became obsolete because the Production Phase work consistently met the standard.
methodology.body_scale: Marks are made at the scale of the full arm and frequently the full torso. The work registers the body that made it at the scale at which the body operates.
methodology.irreversibility: Ink on paper is directionally irreversible. Nothing can be retracted. The direction is always additive. This is not a limitation — it is the method.
curatorial_hierarchy: Qi and vitality over formal resolution
Negative space as consequence of committed action, not design decision
methodology.rating_scale: 1–5 integers. 4 = gallery-tier. 5 = rare. Production Phase standard approximately 1 star harder than Warm-Up Phase.
methodology.dual_authority_rating: Claude (Neon Core) rates cold from image without archive context. Peter confirms or overrides from physical work. Both ratings recorded.

unique_position_statement.summary: Peter Yuill occupies a genuinely unoccupied position in contemporary art practice. No other publicly documented artist combines: (1) a custom multi-model AI intelligence system built around a single career arc, used for archiving and understanding rather than generation; (2) full ambient data transparency — every work correlated against body condition, energy state, movement patterns, and psychological state; (3) a live public archive with quality ratings, kill rates, and studio data updated in real time; (4) a formal quantified destruction practice (the Tithe) with week-by-week kill rates published publicly; (5) large-format gestural ink at the scale of 97×180cm on traditional East Asian papers, in Bangkok, with no institutional framework supporting the practice.
what_he_is_not: Not an AI artist (does not use AI to generate images)
Not a digital artist (works in traditional sumi ink on paper)
Not a data artist (data is documentary, not the medium)
Not sumi-e (traditional Japanese ink painting — his practice is contemporary gestural abstraction using East Asian materials)
what_he_is: A traditional-media painter who has built a sophisticated AI system to archive and understand his practice
A durational artist running a year-long intensive with full public data transparency
One of the most systematically self-documented artists working today
A builder — the technical infrastructure (React site, Railway hosting, Cloudflare R2, MySQL database, multi-model AI system) was built entirely from a mobile phone via Termux
